transcript_events.text: The supervisor toolbar tab should have the input and send buttons docked at the bottom, with the content area scrolling. Also, I noticed that I cleared the log but I don't see the active session doing anything? Help me draft a goal to correct.
Sent 1 queued message to the provider.

I’ll trace the supervisor toolbar’s layout and session/log update path in this standalone worktree, prototype enough to identify the real failure mode, then turn that evidence into a concrete, ambitious goal with verifiable behavior.

The layout issue is already concrete: the supervisor wraps the entire shared chat panel inside an extra conversation container, but that wrapper has no flex sizing, so the composer participates in normal flow instead of remaining at the bottom. I’m now tracing “Clear history” against the live session/event cursor, because the second symptom may be more than a presentation bug.

The second symptom is architectural, not just stale pixels. “Clear history” currently calls the generic chat-clear path, which explicitly stops the supervisor session—even though the Supervisor capability’s own contract says there are no manual lifecycle controls. Meanwhile reconnect restoration is only a ten-event SSE replay and there is no explicit full-session hydration in the toolbar, so an existing session can appear active with an empty or partial transcript. I’m validating that gap with the focused tests and the documented capability contract now.

Focused tests are green, which is useful because it exposes the missing coverage: they only prove restoration from browser local storage, not from the authoritative chat record. I also checked the live durable state. The supposedly inactive session actually received the prompt at 10:56, streamed progress at 10:57, and completed at 10:58; the UI failed to make that work visible. The goal should therefore fix session continuity and observability, not merely add a spinner.

The active session was working; Refine failed to show it reliably. The durable transcript records progress from 10:56–10:58, while the toolbar restores only browser-local history plus a limited SSE replay. “Clear history” also incorrectly stops the Supervisor session through the generic chat-clear path.

### Proposed Goal

**Name:** Make Supervisor chat a persistent, observable dock

**Prompt:**

> Correct the Supervisor toolbar so it behaves as a durable live cockpit rather than a browser-local chat view.
>
> Rework the shared chat layout so the Supervisor summary remains visible, the transcript/activity/queue region owns scrolling, and the input plus Send button remain docked at the bottom at every resized and fullscreen toolbar height. Growing the multiline input must not move the composer off-screen or introduce nested dock scrolling.
>
> Restore Supervisor conversations from authoritative backend chat state rather than depending on local storage or the ten-event SSE replay. When a

The active session was working; Refine failed to show it reliably. The durable transcript records progress from 10:56–10:58, while the toolbar restores only browser-local history plus a limited SSE replay. “Clear history” also incorrectly stops the Supervisor session through the generic chat-clear path.

### Proposed Goal

**Name:** Make Supervisor chat a persistent, observable dock

**Prompt:**

> Correct the Supervisor toolbar so it behaves as a durable live cockpit rather than a browser-local chat view.
>
> Rework the shared chat layout so the Supervisor summary remains visible, the transcript/activity/queue region owns scrolling, and the input plus Send button remain docked at the bottom at every resized and fullscreen toolbar height. Growing the multiline input must not move the composer off-screen or introduce nested dock scrolling.
>
> Restore Supervisor conversations from authoritative backend chat state rather than depending on local storage or the ten-event SSE replay. When attaching, refreshing, reconnecting, or opening another browser, hydrate the transcript, progress, queued messages, closed reason, and real in-flight state. Merge updates by event ID so replay and polling cannot duplicate or omit entries. Preserve any text currently being composed while status polling occurs.
>
> Give Supervisor-specific clearing semantics consistent with its “no manual lifecycle controls” contract. Replace “Clear history” with “Clear view”: it may hide events already seen by that browser, but it must not call the session stop endpoint, discard queued input, terminate the provider, or create a replacement singleton. Events arriving after the clear watermark must immediately appear. A reload should recover the authoritative conversation, with an explicit way to reveal earlier history if the cleared view remains local.
>
> Make active work unmistakable even while the Activity panel is collapsed: show an accurate working/queued/idle state and the latest progress summary, while keeping detailed progress expandable. Provider, authentication, interruption, and capacity-blocked states must remain visible and actionable.
>
> Centralize conversation snapshot and liveness calculation in the shared chat service so API, SSE, CLI-compatible behavior, and browser adapters use one truth. Do not introduce toolbar-owned workflow rules or a second Supervisor scheduler.
>
> Add coverage for:
>
> - measured browser layout at minimum, resized, and fullscreen dock heights;
> - a long transcript and growing textarea with the composer still visible;
> - clearing during an active provider turn without issuing `/stop`;
> - reload/reconnect with empty local storage during an active turn;
> - accurate in-flight state derived from managed operation/process evidence;
> - full transcript recovery beyond the SSE replay limit;
> - event-ID deduplication and preservation of queued messages and draft input;
> - provider failure and session completion after clearing.
>
> Update the Supervisor intent/runbook to remove stale Stop-action guidance and document Clear view, authoritative restoration, and active-state visibility.

Current focused verification is green—10 Supervisor toolbar tests and 10 Rust Supervisor tests—confirming this needs new behavioral coverage rather than repair of an existing failing test. The worktree remains clean.
Provider turn completed.
Also, the activity panel should be collapsed by default.
Sent 1 queued message to the provider.

I’ll trace the activity-panel state from its standalone-chat entry point, confirm whether persistence or responsive behavior affects the default, then implement and verify the smallest shared-state change.

The current branch already contains regression tests describing this exact behavior, so I’m checking whether the implementation landed incompletely or whether a restore path still re-expands the panel. The key distinction is “new default” versus preserving an explicit user expansion.

The prototype confirms the requested behavior is already present in this worktree’s ancestry: new standalone, supervisor, plan, and Goal chats start collapsed; missing legacy state also collapses; and an explicit expansion remains persisted. I’m doing one final targeted sweep for alternate constructors or stale defaults before deciding whether any patch is actually warranted.

The activity panel is already collapsed by default in this worktree via commit `6b258195`.

It applies to standalone, supervisor, plan, and Goal chats, while persisting explicit expansion. Regression coverage is in [web_supervisor_toolbar.test.js](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-standalone-worktrees/19f89772c5097bb51/tests/web_supervisor_toolbar.test.js:232).

Verification: all 10 targeted toolbar tests pass. The worktree remains clean; no additional patch was needed.

Verification: all 10 targeted toolbar tests pass. The worktree remains clean; no additional patch was needed.
Provider turn completed.

The supervisor tab should not have a scrollbar on the window that affects the areas outside the content that grows. I think the default size doesn't account for the extra controls in the supervisor tab (health status), which should be collapsed onto the same line as the session info anyway. My main goal is that the supervisor tab has a scrollbar only in the content-that-grows area.
Sent 1 queued message to the provider.

I’ll trace the supervisor tab’s layout and sizing from the named symptom, then prototype the smallest structural fix that keeps only the growing content scrollable. I’ll also verify the default window-size assumptions and targeted UI behavior before handing it back.

The layout fault is now concrete: the supervisor chat is wrapped in an extra container, so the shared flex rules no longer constrain its transcript. The entire toolbar body therefore becomes the overflow container. I also confirmed the health summary is rendered as a separate row above the chat status, which is exactly the extra vertical cost you suspected.

The prototype now matches the intended ownership boundary: the dock body is fixed and non-scrolling, the supervisor conversation is a shrinkable flex column, and the transcript remains the growing scroll region. Health, lifecycle, work counts, observation timestamp, and session state now share one compact header row; narrower layouts hide only the low-priority process/timestamp labels. Targeted supervisor tests are passing.

Implemented the supervisor scrollbar fix.

- Supervisor health and session information now share one compact header row.
- The toolbar window no longer scrolls; only the growing conversation transcript does.
- Added responsive hiding for secondary status details at narrower widths.
- Documented and regression-tested the layout invariant.

Validation passed: all 17 browser tests, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, and `git diff --check`.

Key changes: [toolbar.js](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-standalone-worktrees/19f89772c5097bb51/src/surfaces/web/static/js/features/toolbar.js:716), [toolbar.css](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-standalone-worktrees/19f89772c5097bb51/src/surfaces/web/static/css/toolbar.css:106), [supervisor test](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-standalone-worktrees/19f89772c5097bb51/tests/web_supervisor_toolbar.test.js:232).

Provider turn completed.

Keep the Supervisor as its existing toolbar tab; do not move it into a different dockable area. Replace the large Queued messages editor panel with normal chat-stream entries: when a message is sent during an active turn, render it immediately in transcript order with a compact pending indicator and a small x control to remove it before dispatch. Pending messages should wrap like ordinary user messages, consume no separate panel or header space, remain editable only if that can be done without expanding the UI, and transition in place from pending to sent without duplication or scroll jumps.
Sent 1 queued message to the provider.

I’ll trace the existing Supervisor tab and queued-message flow first, treating the toolbar location and transcript-order behavior as hard constraints. Then I’ll prototype the smallest shared state/rendering change, exercise it with targeted tests, and tighten any scroll or duplication edge cases I find.

The worktree already contains a focused, uncommitted Supervisor-toolbar change in exactly the four files this request touches. I’m treating that as the current prototype—not overwriting it—and I’m reviewing its behavior and tests before extending it. The first concrete gap is already visible in the existing test expectations: they still assert a separate “Queued messages” panel, so the transcript model and its regression coverage need to move together.

The queue is backend-owned, and the important unknown is now closed: Refine batches all queued inputs into one provider turn, but it also writes that batch as one synthetic user transcript event. That canonical shape would prevent true in-place transitions for multiple queued bubbles. I’m going to preserve provider batching while recording each queued input as its own transcript event; the UI can then reconcile one pending bubble to one sent bubble without inventing a second history model.

queued_messages.text: Broaden this into one shared CLI-agent interaction model for Supervisor, attached chats, and Standalone. Keep input docked and accept submission at any time. During an active turn show Working with elapsed time and Esc to interrupt. Let Up and Down navigate queued and previously submitted prompts. Put submitted messages immediately into transcript order with a compact pending state and x removal before dispatch; do not use a separate queue panel. Keep typing, rendering, scrolling, and keyboard controls fast and responsive. Reuse shared behavior across surfaces and preserve backend queue semantics and accessibility.
